steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The example must show that elevation and prominence can differ significantly for one mountain. We need one mountain whose two values are far apart.

steps.title: Pick the data point
steps.content:
Choice A: Saser Kangri III — elevation $7{,}495$ m vs. prominence only $850$ m. That's a huge gap, perfectly illustrating the elevation/prominence distinction.

B and C compare two mountains, not the two metrics for one mountain. D gives only elevation. Only A directly shows the within-mountain disparity.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
